revision.text:
{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)}}
and
{{ill|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Ideogramm}}{{Cite book |author={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig}} |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
{{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}} († ca. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet}}
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum}}
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen|12=draft|12=draft}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=draft|12=draft}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=draft|12=draft}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories|}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

and
{{ill|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Ideogramm|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book |author={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=draft|12=draft}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet

the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
{{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}} († ca. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=draft|12=draft}}. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

revision.text:
{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)|12=draft|12=draft}}
and
{{ill|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Ideogramm|12=draft|12=draft}}{{Cite book |author={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=draft|12=draft}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
{{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}} († ca. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=draft|12=draft}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}}
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft|12=draft}}
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen|12=draft|12=draft}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft|12=draft}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft|12=draft}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=draft|12=draft}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft|12=draft}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=draft|12=draft}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

{{ill|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Ideogramm|12=draft}}{{Cite book |author={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig|12=draft}} |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=draft}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
{{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft}} († ca. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)|12=draft}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=draft}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}}
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft}}
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen|12=draft}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius|12=draft}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=draft}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=draft}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=draft}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=draft}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}}

{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)}}
and
{{ill|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Ideogramm}}{{Cite book |author={{ill|Rainer Hannig|de|Rainer Hannig}} |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
{{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}} († ca. 1460 BC), {{ill|Thebes, Egypt|de|Theben (Ägypten)}}, {{ill|TT353|de|TT353}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet}}
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum}}
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages without German translation box]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:11]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}}

Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for {{ill|Egyptian hieroglyphs|de|Ägyptische Hieroglyphen|12=draft}}, but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the {{ill|Ramesseum|de|Ramesseum|12=draft}}.{{Cite book |editor={{ill|Karl Richard Lepsius|de|Karl Richard Lepsius|12=draft}} |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of {{ill|Season of the Inundation|lt=Akhet|de|Achet|12=draft}}''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=draft}}''', making it the second month of the year.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=draft}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=draft}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of {{ill|Senenmut|de|Senenmut|12=draft}}'''. According to {{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.{{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}}: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=draft}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=draft}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=draft}} |editor={{ill|Academy of Sciences and Literature|de|Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|12=draft}} |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author={{ill|Richard Anthony Parker|de|Richard Anthony Parker|12=draft}} |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Menchet (Month)]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]] [[Category:Monat des altägyptischen Kalenders]]

revision.text:
{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|qq=}}
and
[[:en:Ideogram|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)]]{{Cite book |author=[[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]] |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|qq=}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
[[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ca. 1460 BC), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]]
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Calendar Months]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]]

[[:en:Ideogram|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)]]{{Cite book |author=[[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]] |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|qq=}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
[[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ca. 1460 BC), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]]
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] [[sn:Menchet (month)]]

(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}}

This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}}

{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}
and
[[:en:Ideogram|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)]]{{Cite book |author=[[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]] |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|qq=|WD=Q1418022}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
[[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ca. 1460 BC), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|qq=|WD=Q666212}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]]
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|qq=|WD=Q1944246}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|qq=|WD=Q1279717}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|qq=|WD=Q1920275}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|qq=|WD=Q108862211}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|qq=|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}}

[[de:Menchet (Monat)]]
{| class="center" style="width:320px;" ! colspan="3"|Clothing |- |{{ill|Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|de|Determinativ (Ägyptologie)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Determinative (Egyptian hieroglyphs)|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}
and
[[:en:Ideogram|Ideogram (Egyptian hieroglyphs)]]{{Cite book |author=[[:en:Rainer Hannig|Rainer Hannig]] |title=The Language of the Pharaohs |title-extra=Great German-Egyptian Dictionary (2800–950 BC) |series=Cultural History of the Ancient World |volume=86 |publisher=von Zabern |location=Mainz |date=2000-02-01 |pages=845 |DNB=957877323}} | class="hiero" |{{ill|Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|de|Gardiner-Liste#S – Kronen|S27|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardiner's sign list#S – Crowns|WD=Q1418022}} | rowspan="4" | Menchet
''mnḫt'' |- |Determinative | class="hiero" |Y1:Z2 |- |Name of
the month | class="hiero" |Y5:N35 Aa1:X1 |- |In the ceiling painting
in the tomb of
[[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]Calendar division in the astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]] († ca. 1460 BC), [[:en:Thebes, Egypt|Thebes, Egypt]], {{ill|TT353|de|TT353|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/TT353|WD=Q666212}}. A high-resolution version is available [https://images.metmuseum.org/CRDImages/eg/original/48.105.52_EGDP012289.jpg at the Metropolitan Museum of Art] in New York. (Retrieved November 14, 2021)
(Reading direction:
right to left) |style="padding:0; width:135px;"| {{Annotated Image | image = Senenmut-Grab.JPG | image-width = 1150 | width = 135 | height = 140 | image-left = -815 | image-top = -140 | noframe = 1 | outer-css = margin:0; }} |- ! colspan="3"|The Month in the Seasons |- | Number and
Season | class="hiero" |N11:Z1*Z1 SA:x*t | rowspan="2" | 2nd month
of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]
''2-nw-aḫt'' |- | Same as above, in
the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]]
(mirrored) | class="hiero" |N11:N14-N11:Z1*Z1:N35-SA:t*N33 |- | colspan="3" |
Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|WD=Q1944246}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|WD=Q1279717}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|WD=Q1920275}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|WD=Q108862211}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}}

Wikipedia currently defaults to a '''left-to-right''' writing direction for [[:en:Egyptian hieroglyphs|Egyptian hieroglyphs]], but the predominant direction in columns or rows—also seen in the image excerpt—is '''right to left''', as in the ceiling painting of the [[:en:Ramesseum|Ramesseum]].{{Cite book |editor=[[:en:Karl Richard Lepsius|Karl Richard Lepsius]] |title=Monuments of the New Kingdom |series=Monuments from Egypt and Ethiopia after the drawings of the scientific expedition sent by His Majesty the King of Prussia, Friedrich Wilhelm IV, to these lands in the years 1842–1845 |volume=Volume VI, containing Part III |publisher=Nicolaische Buchhandlung |location=Berlin |date= |pages=171 |online=https://edoc3.bibliothek.uni-halle.de/lepsius/tafelwa3.html |scope=Plate XCI-CLXXII}}
Note: |- |}
'''Menchet''' ''mnḫt'', English: '''Clothing''', was the name of the '''second month of [[:en:Season of the Inundation|Akhet]]''' in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|de|Mondkalender (Altes Ägypten)|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|1=Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian lunar calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Lunar Calendar|WD=Q1944246}}''', making it the second month of the year.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 45, §230 / Table 7. Menchet followed the month of {{ill|Techi (month)|de|Techi (Monat)|1=Techi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi (month)|13=User:Immanuelle/Techi|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} in both the '''{{ill|Ebers calendar|de|Ebers-Kalender|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebers calendar|WD=Q1279717}}''' and the '''astronomical ceiling painting in the tomb of [[:en:Senenmut|Senenmut]]'''. According to [[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] and other scholars, months were originally named after '''the most significant festivals''' celebrated during them.[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]]: ''The Calendars of Ancient Egypt.'' In: ''Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization.'' Vol. 26. Chicago (Illinois) 1950, p. 58, §290. This was likely the case for '''Menchet''', whose name was probably taken from the '''{{ill|Menchet Festival|de|Menchet-Fest|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Menchet Festival|WD=Q1920275}}''', before the festival’s date was fixed in the '''{{ill|Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|de|Ägyptischer Verwaltungskalender|1=Egyptian civil calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian civil calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian civil calendar|WD=Q108862211}}'''. In this system, all '''annual cycle festivals''' were tied to a specific month and only celebrated once a year.{{Cite book |author={{ill|Siegfried Schott|de|Siegfried Schott|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Siegfried Schott|WD=Q102228}} |editor=[[:en:Akademie der Wissenschaften und der Literatur|Academy of Sciences and Literature]] |title=Ancient Egyptian Festival Dates |series=Proceedings of the Humanities and Social Sciences Class |number=10 |location=Mainz |date=1950-10-27 |pages=46 f.}} == References == == Further Reading == * {{Cite book |author=[[:en:Richard Anthony Parker|Richard Anthony Parker]] |title=The Calendars of Ancient Egypt |series=Studies in Ancient Oriental Civilization |volume=26 |publisher=The University of Chicago Press |location=Chicago (Illinois) |date=1950 |language=en}} : {{Ancient Egyptian Lunar Months}} {{translated page|de|Menchet (Monat)|version=251558568|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[de:Menchet (Monat)]] [[Category:Months of the Ancient Egyptian Calendars]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata no English label]] {{wikidata link|Q1624441}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
